senses.definition: plural of caravels, small sailing ships used in the 15th–16th centuries with two or three masts and lateen sails
examples: The explorers sailed to the new world aboard caravels.
Several caravels rushed through the harbor during the festival.

senses.definition: a spice made from the seeds of the caraway plant, with a warm, sweet flavor
examples: She added caraway to the rye bread dough.
Caraway seeds have a distinctive, mild anise-like aroma.
synonyms: persian cumin? (note: avoid mislabel; caraway is distinct)

senses.definition: plural form of caraway; seeds of the caraway plant used as spice
examples: The recipe calls for two tablespoons of caraways.

senses.definition: a proper noun; historically a name related to a place or person in certain languages; may be a dialect form or misspelling
examples: The map listed Carayan as a historic site.

senses.definition: short for carbohydrate; a nutrient group providing energy from sugars, starches, and fibers
examples: Pasta is a high-carb food.
Bodybuilders track carbs as part of their diet.

senses.definition: a slang or informal term for a visible sleeping lump or lump in the mouth? (Note: ambiguous; use with caution)
examples: No clear secondary sense widely used in contemporary English.

senses.definition: a synthetic cholinergic drug used in ophthalmology and pharmacology to mimic acetylcholine; stimulates muscarinic and nicotinic receptors
examples: Carbachol is used to induce miosis during eye surgery.

senses.definition: plural of carbachol; multiple carbachol drugs or solutions
examples: The vial contained several carbachols for the procedure.

senses.definition: a hypothetical or fictional instrument to measure carboxyl groups or carbon content; not a standard term
examples: In the lab, a carbacidometer would record carbon content.

senses.definition: a chemical compound derived from carbamic acid; often used as a pesticide or in polymers
examples: Carbamates are used in pesticide formulations.

senses.definition: a salt or ester of carbamic acid in chemistry
examples: The reaction produced a stable carbamate.

senses.definition: plural of carbamate; chemical compounds derived from carbamic acid
examples: Pesticide formulations often contain carbamates.

senses.definition: a medication used primarily to treat epilepsy and certain nerve pain disorders
examples: Carbamazepine is prescribed for trigeminal neuralgia.

senses.definition: plural form of carbamazepine
examples: Several carbamazepines were tested in the trial.

senses.definition: relating to or containing the carbamoyl group (−NH−CO−) in chemistry
examples: Carbamic acid derivatives were studied.

senses.definition: a compound also known as urea, produced in the liver and used in various industrial processes
examples: Carbamide is another name for urea used in fertilizers.

senses.definition: plural of carbamide; compounds containing a carbamamide group
examples: Researchers synthesized several different carbamides.

senses.definition: a chemical compound used as a diamidine antimicrobial agent
examples: Carbamidine has been explored for medicinal use.

senses.definition: plural of carbamidines; chemical compounds containing the amidine group connected to carbamyl
examples: The study examined several carbamidines.

senses.definition: a prefix in chemistry indicating a carbamido group attached to another moiety
examples: carbamido-ethyl compound

senses.definition: an organomanganese or organometallic compound? (note: token not widely used; may be historical/rare)
examples: Historical texts mention carbamine in early chemistry experiments.

senses.definition: a chemical prefix indicating a carbamoyl group attached to an organic molecule
examples: carbamino-containing compound

senses.definition: a functional group in chemistry consisting of a carbonyl group linked to an amide
examples: The molecule features a carbamoyl group.

senses.definition: plural of carbamoyl
examples: The reaction produced several carbamoyls.

senses.definition: a chemical group containing the −N−CO− moiety; used in biochemistry and chemistry
examples: Carbamyl groups occur in some urea-containing molecules.

senses.definition: plural of carbamyl
examples: Different carbamyls were observed in the sample.

senses.definition: a chemical compound; an aniline-derived lid or fragrance compound? (Note: carbanil is a historical or obsolete term; usage is rare)
examples: Carbanil was referenced in older chemical catalogues.
